skill.name: journal-impact-factor-trend
skill.description: Show journal impact factor and quartile trends over 5 years to help researchers select publication venues.
skill.summary: Analyze 5-year impact factor trends and quartile rankings for academic journals.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 47 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are triggered by substring matches (e.g., 'MD' in 'Nature Medicine') in documentation and a non-crypto argparse usage. The 31 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code fences in documentation, not executable commands. The path traversal findings at SKILL.md:120,123 are security checklist items documenting mitigations. One minor concern exists: scripts/main.py line 94 reads a file from a user-supplied path argument without validation, but this is read-only in a sandboxed environment. No evidence of malware, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Unvalidated file path in --journal-list argument
low_findings.description: scripts/main.py line 94 reads a file from the user-supplied --journal-list argument without path traversal validation. A malicious user could supply a path like '../../etc/passwd' to read arbitrary files. Risk is low because the skill runs in a sandboxed environment and only reads file contents.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm (false positive - dismissed)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at 5 locations. All are false positives. The findings at scripts/main.py:78 (argparse.ArgumentParser), SKILL.md:3 (license field), SKILL.md:24 (documentation text), SKILL.md:94 (parameter table), and audit_result_v2.json:72 (evaluation score note) contain no cryptographic operations. The scanner likely matched on the substring 'MD' appearing in text like 'Nature Medicine' and 'Medicine, Research & Experimental'.

low_findings.title: Ruby/shell backtick execution (false positive - dismissed)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 31 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. All are false positives. The flagged content in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md consists of markdown code fences (```bash, ```text) and inline backtick code blocks showing example Python commands. These are documentation, not executable Ruby backtick operations. The commands shown (python -m py_compile, python scripts/main.py) are legitimate Python invocations for the skill's purpose.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance (false positive - dismissed)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 7 instances of 'system reconnaissance'. All are false positives. The findings in SKILL.md:58 (output discipline guidance), SKILL.md:70 (audit-ready commands section), and SKILL.md:143 (test case heading) are standard documentation content. The findings in audit_result_v2.json:155,160,190,225 are evaluation notes about scope boundaries. None indicate reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Path traversal sequence (false positive - dismissed)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 2 instances of 'path traversal sequence' at SKILL.md:120 and SKILL.md:123. Both are false positives. These lines are security checklist items that document path traversal mitigations: 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'. The checklist mentions '../' as a pattern to guard against, not as actual code performing traversal.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL (false positive - dismissed)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a 'hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md:7. This is a false positive. The URL is a citation linking to the GitHub source repository: https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills. It is not an executable network call or data exfiltration endpoint. The scripts/main.py source code contains no network requests.

content.user_title: Track Journal Impact Factor Trends and Quartile Rankings
content.value_statement: Researchers need to identify rising or declining journals when choosing where to publish their work. This skill analyzes 5-year impact factor trends and quartile rankings to support publication strategy decisions.
actual_capabilities: Display 5-year impact factor trend data for target journals
Show quartile ranking changes over the analysis period
Classify journal trajectory as rising, stable, or declining
Analyze single journals or batch process from a list
Generate formatted trend reports with growth percentages
limitations: Does not connect to live Journal Citation Reports or real citation databases
Hardcoded sample data included for demonstration purposes only
Cannot compare across different research categories
Limited to basic trend calculation and does not predict future values

use_cases.title: Choosing a Publication Venue
use_cases.description: Compare impact factor trends across multiple target journals to identify which ones are rising in influence.

use_cases.title: Journal Performance Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track quartile ranking changes for journals in a specific field over a 5-year period.

use_cases.title: Literature Review Quality Assessment
use_cases.description: Quickly assess whether journals cited in a literature review are stable or declining in impact.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Journal Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the impact factor trend for the journal [Journal Name] over the last 5 years.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the impact factor trends for all journals listed in this file: [path/to/journals.txt]

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the 5-year impact factor trends for [Journal A] and [Journal B] and tell me which one is rising faster.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Output Format
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the impact factor trend for [Journal Name] for the last [N] years and output the results as a formatted table with growth percentages.

output_examples.input: Show me the impact factor trend for Nature Medicine
output_examples.output:
Journal: Nature Medicine | Category: Medicine, Research & Experimental
Current IF: 82.90 | 5 Years Ago: 36.13
Growth: +129.5% | Trend: Rising Star | Current Quartile: Q1

output_examples.input: Analyze the trends for Nature Medicine, Cell, and NEJM
output: Nature Medicine: IF 82.90, Growth +129.5%, Trend: Rising Star, Q1
Cell: IF 64.50, Growth +105.7%, Trend: Rising Star, Q1
NEJM: IF 120.70, Growth +61.6%, Trend: Rising Star, Q1

output_examples.input: Compare the 5-year trends for Cell and NEJM
output_examples.output: Both Cell and NEJM show strong upward trajectories and remain in Q1. Cell grew +105.7% from 31.40 to 64.50. NEJM grew +61.6% from 74.70 to 120.70. Both are classified as Rising Star.

best_practices: Verify the journal name exactly matches the name in Journal Citation Reports for accurate data
Use the batch list option when evaluating multiple candidate journals for a manuscript
Review both growth percentage and quartile ranking together for a complete picture
anti_patterns: Do not use impact factor trends as the sole criterion for journal selection
Do not compare impact factors across different research categories
Do not interpret past trends as guaranteed future performance predictions

faq.question: What data source does this skill use?
faq.answer: This skill uses sample data for demonstration. A production deployment should connect to Journal Citation Reports or a bibliometric database.

faq.question: Can I analyze any journal?
faq.answer: Only journals present in the included database will return results. The current sample includes Nature Medicine, Cell, and NEJM.

faq.question: How is the trend classification determined?
faq.answer: Growth over 20% is Rising Star, 5% to 20% is Growing, -5% to 5% is Stable, and below -5% is Declining.

faq.question: Can I customize the analysis period?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --years parameter to set the number of years to analyze. The default is 5 years.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports table output by default and can generate plots when the --output plot parameter is used.

faq.question: Does this skill make external API calls?
faq.answer: No. All data processing happens locally with the included data. Network access is not required.
